(Portuguese pronunciation: [[ˈbaʁɐ dɐ tʃiˈʒukɐ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Portuguese "Help:IPA/Portuguese")) (usually known as **Barra**) is an upper-middle class neighborhood or [bairro](/wiki/Bairro "Bairro") in the [Southwest Zone](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro#West_Zone "Rio de Janeiro") of [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"), [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil"), located in the western portion of the city on the [Atlantic Ocean](/wiki/Atlantic_Ocean "Atlantic Ocean"). Barra is well known for its beaches, its many lakes and rivers, and its lifestyle. This neighbourhood represents 4.7% of the city population and 13% of the total area of Rio de Janeiro.

**Barra da Tijuca** (Portuguese pronunciation: [[ˈbaʁɐ dɐ tʃiˈʒukɐ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Portuguese "Help:IPA/Portuguese")) (usually known as **Barra**) is an upper-middle class neighborhood or [bairro](/wiki/Bairro "Bairro") in the [Southwest Zone](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro#West_Zone "Rio de Janeiro") of [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"), [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil"), located in the western portion of the city on the [Atlantic Ocean](/wiki/Atlantic_Ocean "Atlantic Ocean"). Barra is well known for its beaches, its many lakes and rivers, and its lifestyle. This neighbourhood represents 4.7% of the city population and 13% of the total area of Rio de Janeiro.

Barra da Tijuca is classified as one of the most developed places in Brazil, with one of the highest [Human Development Indexes](/wiki/Human_Development_Index "Human Development Index") (HDI) in the country, as measured in the 2000 Brazil Census. Unlike the South Zone and Rio's Downtown, Barra da Tijuca, built only 30 years ago, follows the [Modernist](/wiki/Modernist "Modernist") standards, with large boulevards creating the major transit axis. The area's masterplan was designed by [Lúcio Costa](/wiki/L%C3%BAcio_Costa "Lúcio Costa"), known for his work on [Brasília](/wiki/Bras%C3%ADlia "Brasília"), and creates a region filled with many gardens, shopping malls, apartment buildings and large mansions. In recent years, due to the rapid development of the [Brazilian economy](/wiki/Economy_of_Brazil "Economy of Brazil"), Barra's population has increased by over 100,000, as a large number of residents and companies search for cheaper real estate as an alternative to the densely populated South Zone of Rio. Demographic data indicates that the region is the fastest growing neighborhood in Rio: 98,851 in 1991, 174,353 in 2000, and 300,823 in 2010.

Barra natives and residents are known as Barristas, or more popularly, Barrenses. The neighborhood is a cultural, economic, and administrative hub of the city, and is believed to be the safest of Rio's [upper-class](/wiki/Social_apartheid_in_Brazil "Social apartheid in Brazil") neighbourhoods because of its lack of [favelas](/wiki/Favela "Favela") and plentiful private and public security. It is the home of several celebrities and football players, such as the singer [Anitta](/wiki/Anitta_(singer) "Anitta (singer)"). In August 2016, Barra hosted most of the venues of the [2016 Summer Olympics](/wiki/2016_Summer_Olympics "2016 Summer Olympics"), the first held in [South America](/wiki/South_America "South America").

## Etymology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Barra_da_Tijuca&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Etymology")]

The name Barra da Tijuca can be roughly translated as "clay sandbank". *Barra* means port entrance or [sandbank](/wiki/Sandbank "Sandbank"), and *Tijuca* is a word originally from the [Tupi](/wiki/Tupi_language "Tupi language") *ty-yúc* and means putrid water, mud, [swamp](/wiki/Swamp "Swamp"), puddle, clay or clay-pit.
*Tijuca* (meaning [marsh](/wiki/Marsh "Marsh") or [swamp](/wiki/Swamp "Swamp")) from *ty* ("water") and *îuká* ("to kill").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Barra_da_Tijuca&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/17/Barra_da_Tijuca_nos_anos_50.jpg/250px-Barra_da_Tijuca_nos_anos_50.jpg)](/wiki/File:Barra_da_Tijuca_nos_anos_50.jpg)

Barra da Tijuca in the 1950s

The region of Barra da Tijuca was originally a large beach, with typical undergrowth sandbanks. The area, full of swamps and unsuitable for planting, remained unoccupied until the middle of the twentieth century, even though occasional groups of fishermen frequented the region. In 1667, the region was given to religious Benedictines, who settled only in the neighborhoods of [Camorim](/wiki/Camorim,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Camorim, Rio de Janeiro"), [Vargem Pequena](/wiki/Vargem_Pequena,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Vargem Pequena, Rio de Janeiro"), and [Vargem Grande](/wiki/Vargem_Grande,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Vargem Grande, Rio de Janeiro"). In 1900, the lands of Barra da Tijuca and Baixada Jacarepaguá were sold to the company Remedial Territorial Agricultural and SA, ESTA, which remains a large land owner in the area. The concentration of large tracts of land in the hands of a few was one of the causes of its late growth. Additionally it is separated from the rest of the municipality by large, difficult-to-cross mountain ranges with peaks ranging from 800–1200 meters.

Development of the area took place initially on its two ends, in current [Jardim Oceânico](/wiki/Jardim_Oce%C3%A2nico,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Jardim Oceânico, Rio de Janeiro") and in [Recreio dos Bandeirantes](/wiki/Recreio_dos_Bandeirantes,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Recreio dos Bandeirantes, Rio de Janeiro"). A bridge was then built by private initiative over the Tijuca Lagoon to serve the area's new inhabitants. Significant early development of Barra da Tijuca occurred during the administration of Governor Negrão de Lima, the former governor of the [state of Guanabara](/wiki/Guanabara_(state) "Guanabara (state)"), who commissioned Lúcio Costa, one of the region's urban designers. The plan for Barra in 1969 was similar to the earlier one for Brasília. It was inspired by American urban planning style with wide boulevards and large open spaces.

In the 1970s the [Lagoa–Barra Highway](/wiki/Lagoa%E2%80%93Barra_Highway "Lagoa–Barra Highway") was built, which allowed a greater development since it reduced the time to go to the South Zone of the city of Rio. At the same time, large planned condominiums were developed in Barra, such as the condominiums Nova Ipanema and Novo Leblon. In the 1990s, another large urban development that enabled better connection with the [North Zone](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro#North_Zone "Rio de Janeiro") of Rio was the creation of the [Yellow Line](/wiki/Yellow_Line_(Rio_de_Janeiro) "Yellow Line (Rio de Janeiro)"), an expressway linking Barra da Tijuca to the Galeão International Airport. Since then, the growth of Barra da Tijuca has been characterized by large inflows of people from all parts of the metropolitan region of Rio de Janeiro.

### Barra City's Project

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Barra_da_Tijuca&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Barra City's Project")]

During the 1980s, Barra da Tijuca experienced a population explosion, with virtually all the land along its boulevards occupied by large residential condominiums, parks, supermarkets, shopping malls, schools and hospitals. The avenues were widened and received traffic lights. At this time there was a movement for the declaration of Barra as a separate city; While there was a referendum to this effect, and a majority of voters voted to make it a separate city, turnout was too low for it to succeed. [*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

There is still a bill in progress in the Legislative Assembly of Rio de Janeiro for the formation of a new Barra da Tijuca council from the region's districts (Barra, [Recreio](/wiki/Recreio_dos_Bandeirantes,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Recreio dos Bandeirantes, Rio de Janeiro"), [Grumari](/wiki/Grumari,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Grumari, Rio de Janeiro"), [Vargem Pequena](/wiki/Vargem_Pequena,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Vargem Pequena, Rio de Janeiro"), [Vargem Grande](/wiki/Vargem_Grande,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Vargem Grande, Rio de Janeiro"), [Itanhangá](/wiki/Itanhang%C3%A1,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Itanhangá, Rio de Janeiro"), [Joá](/wiki/Jo%C3%A1,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Joá, Rio de Janeiro") e [Camorim](/wiki/Camorim,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Camorim, Rio de Janeiro")). The project, however, depends on the approval of the Federal Congressional bill PEC 13/03, which transfers to the states the power to legislate on this matter, as it was until 1996.

## Lifestyle

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Barra_da_Tijuca&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Lifestyle")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a6/Peninsula_RJ_2019.jpg/330px-Peninsula_RJ_2019.jpg)](/wiki/File:Peninsula_RJ_2019.jpg)

Península's residential towers.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/09/Barra_da_Tijuca.jpg/330px-Barra_da_Tijuca.jpg)](/wiki/File:Barra_da_Tijuca.jpg)

Jardim Oceânico

As the most recent region, built only about 30 years ago, Barra introduced a way of living characterized by large luxury condominiums with leisure infrastructure (sports courts, pools, private groves and lakes, spas, gyms) inside of the condominium for the use of its residents and guests. The "neighborhood-condos", as they were named, have the idea of creating an exclusive neighborhood for its residents, making it possible for them to live a complete life without the need to leave the condominium. The condos also have a high security system to ensure the privacy and safety of its residents. The residential areas of Barra are also known as being [environmentally friendly](/wiki/Environmentally_friendly "Environmentally friendly").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

The region is characterized by a [car culture](/wiki/Car_culture "Car culture"), and is crossed by three main routes: the Avenue of the America - "Avenida das Américas" (the main road in the region, approximately 21 km long), "Avenida Ayrton Senna" (which connects the district of Barra and the Yellow Line, or '[Linha Amarela](/wiki/Linha_Amarela "Linha Amarela")' highway) and "Avenida Lúcio Costa", formerly Sernambetiba Avenue, which follows the coastline.

### Jardim Oceânico

[Jardim Oceânico](/wiki/Jardim_Oce%C3%A2nico,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Jardim Oceânico, Rio de Janeiro") ([Portuguese](/wiki/Portuguese_language "Portuguese language"): Ocean Garden) is a subdistrict of Barra da Tijuca. Predominantly formed by three-story buildings It is an area similar to the South Zone of Rio de Janeiro. As part of the preparations for the Rio 2016 Olympics, a subway station was opened in the neighborhood. The mainly residential area is also home to several bars and restaurants on the Olegario Maciel and Erico Verissimo streets.

### Península

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Barra_da_Tijuca&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Península")]

Main article: [Península (Rio de Janeiro)](/wiki/Pen%C3%ADnsula_(Rio_de_Janeiro) "Península (Rio de Janeiro)")

[Península](/wiki/Pen%C3%ADnsula_(Rio_de_Janeiro) "Península (Rio de Janeiro)") was designed by a real estate company, the original project being the creation of a new environmentally friendly neighborhood called Península Barra. However, the project evolved from being a neighborhood to a massive private urban development complex, making the Península (which has the same size of Leblon's neighborhood) the newest and first eco-friendly urban development complex in [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"). Located in the heart of Barra, right behind the [Barra Shopping](/wiki/Barra_Shopping "Barra Shopping") in an area surrounded by its own private lake (Península Bay) and extensive leisure infrastructure, Península won the prize of the best urban development complex in Brazil. The complex, which still has some towers under construction, will consist at its completion of 62 residential towers, two business towers, a mini mall, five theme gardens and two large parks. The real estate boom in Rio after the city was chosen for the 2016 Summer Olympics affected the prices in Península; researches show that the prices have increased up to 300%, making the square meter in Península one of the most expensive in the city of [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"). The complex, considered one of the best family urban developments in the city,[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] is now one of the favorite places of the celebrities of [Rede Globo](/wiki/Rede_Globo "Rede Globo").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Barra_da_Tijuca&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Education")]

Several universities have a [campus](/wiki/Campus "Campus") in the district, including [Universidade Estácio de Sá](/wiki/Est%C3%A1cio_de_S%C3%A1_University "Estácio de Sá University"), [Pontifícia Universidade Católica](/wiki/Pontifical_Catholic_University_of_Rio_de_Janeiro "Pontifical Catholic University of Rio de Janeiro"), [Universidade Gama Filho](/wiki/Gama_Filho_University "Gama Filho University"), [Universidade Veiga de Almeida](/wiki/Veiga_de_Almeida_University "Veiga de Almeida University"), [Centro Universitário IBMR-Laureate](/w/index.php?title=Centro_Universit%C3%A1rio_IBMR-Laureate&action=edit&redlink=1 "Centro Universitário IBMR-Laureate (page does not exist)") and [Instituto Brasileiro de Mercado de Capitais](/wiki/Instituto_Brasileiro_de_Mercado_de_Capitais "Instituto Brasileiro de Mercado de Capitais"). Schools in the district include the [Escola Suíço-Brasileira Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Escola_Su%C3%AD%C3%A7o-Brasileira_Rio_de_Janeiro "Escola Suíço-Brasileira Rio de Janeiro").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Transportation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Barra_da_Tijuca&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Transportation")]

There are three main avenues in Barra: [Avenida das Américas](/w/index.php?title=Avenida_das_Am%C3%A9ricas&action=edit&redlink=1 "Avenida das Américas (page does not exist)") (which connects almost the whole area of Barra), [Avenida Ayrton Senna](/w/index.php?title=Avenida_Ayrton_Senna&action=edit&redlink=1 "Avenida Ayrton Senna (page does not exist)") (formerly Avenida Alvorada, which connects Barra to [Jacarepaguá](/wiki/Jacarepagu%C3%A1,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Jacarepaguá, Rio de Janeiro") neighbourhood) and [Avenida Lúcio costa](/w/index.php?title=Avenida_L%C3%BAcio_costa&action=edit&redlink=1 "Avenida Lúcio costa (page does not exist)") (formerly [Avenida Sernambetiba](/w/index.php?title=Avenida_Sernambetiba&action=edit&redlink=1 "Avenida Sernambetiba (page does not exist)"), which passes alongside the beach). The connection works of Barra with the rest of the urban network are among the most expensive works already carried out in Rio. Barra has many bus routes and in 2009, Barra's subway line started to be built for the 2016 Rio Summer Olympics. [Jacarepaguá Airport](/wiki/Jacarepagu%C3%A1_Airport "Jacarepaguá Airport"), an airport specialized in [general aviation](/wiki/General_aviation "General aviation"), is also located in the neighborhood.

## Sports

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Barra_da_Tijuca&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Sports")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/47/CampoOlimpicodeGolfe.jpg/250px-CampoOlimpicodeGolfe.jpg)](/wiki/File:CampoOlimpicodeGolfe.jpg)

The [Rio de Janeiro Olympic golf course](/wiki/Olympic_golf_course_(Rio_de_Janeiro) "Olympic golf course (Rio de Janeiro)")

* The [Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu](/wiki/Brazilian_Jiu-Jitsu "Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu") academy [Gracie Barra](/wiki/Gracie_Barra "Gracie Barra") originated in Barra and is named after it.
* The [2007 Pan American Games](/wiki/2007_Pan_American_Games "2007 Pan American Games") and [2016 Summer Olympics and Paralympics](/wiki/Venues_of_the_2016_Summer_Olympics_and_Paralympics "Venues of the 2016 Summer Olympics and Paralympics") took place for the most part in Barra, the latter featuring 12 permanent and three temporary venues.
* The [football (soccer)](/wiki/Football_(soccer) "Football (soccer)") club [Barra da Tijuca Futebol Clube](/w/index.php?title=Barra_da_Tijuca_Futebol_Clube&action=edit&redlink=1 "Barra da Tijuca Futebol Clube (page does not exist)") derives its name from the region. Its headquarters are located in neighbouring [Camorim](/wiki/Camorim,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Camorim, Rio de Janeiro").
* Three major Associations' headquarters are located in Barra da Tijuca: the [Brazilian Football Confederation](/wiki/Brazilian_Football_Confederation "Brazilian Football Confederation"), the [Brazilian Volleyball Confederation](/wiki/Confedera%C3%A7%C3%A3o_Brasileira_de_Voleibol "Confederação Brasileira de Voleibol") and [Brazilian Olympic Committee](/wiki/Brazilian_Olympic_Committee "Brazilian Olympic Committee").
* The *Golden Green Golf Club* provides three-par six-hole court greens open to outside players with illumination for night play. *Itanhanga Golf Club* is also nearby.
* Surf competitions, such as Rio Marathon Surf Internacional, Festival Petrobras de Surfe, Campeonato Velox Surf Amador and Circuito Petrobras are held in Barra. There are several surfing schools in the neighbourhood, an evidence of the popularity of this sport with the locals.
* The Aero Clube do Brasil provides parachuting experiences. The *Rio Sport Center* offers tennis courts to the public. *Barrashopping* and *Barra Square* are home to bowling alleys.
* There are several places where beach and court volleyball are practised. The Bernandinho's School is located in Barra.
* The numerous [condos](/wiki/Condominium_(living_space) "Condominium (living space)") also offer several others sports courts exclusively for their residents.

## Beach

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Barra_da_Tijuca&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Beach")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/31/Parque_Natural_Municipal_da_Prainha_-_panoramio.jpg/250px-Parque_Natural_Municipal_da_Prainha_-_panoramio.jpg)](/wiki/File:Parque_Natural_Municipal_da_Prainha_-_panoramio.jpg)

Prainha beach

The 18 km long beach is the largest of Rio de Janeiro's beaches. Barra's beach starts at Morro do Joá and ends at the [Recreio dos Bandeirantes](/wiki/Recreio_dos_Bandeirantes "Recreio dos Bandeirantes") neighborhood, in Pontal de Sernambetiba, beyond Avenida Lúcio Costa. Most of its waters are clear and green, and have an uncommon wave formation.[*[clarification needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Please_clarify "Wikipedia:Please clarify")*] Barra da Tijuca beach is one of the most sought after beaches by surfers, windsurfers, bodyboarders, kitesurfers and fishing enthusiasts. There is also a [cycling lane](/wiki/Segregated_cycle_facilities "Segregated cycle facilities") along the beach. Pepê beach, closer do Morro do Joá, has kite surf schools, food kiosks, and surf schools.

## Culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Barra_da_Tijuca&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Culture")]

### Teatro dos Grandes Atores

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Barra_da_Tijuca&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Teatro dos Grandes Atores")]

The Teatro dos Grandes Atores (Great Actors Theater) is located in the Shopping Barra Square mall. It has a small 35 square metres (380 sq ft) foyer, and two auditoriums, each with 396 seats, the Blue Room and the Red Room. Both have good acoustics, and balconies that give good views of the stage. The theater opened in 1995 with Aparecida Margarida, with Marília Pêra, in the Blue Room and A Era do Rádio directed by Sérgio Britto in the Red Room.[[4]](#cite_note-4)
*Ricardo Torres* was staged here in 1996.[[5]](#cite_note-Albuquerque2004-5)
Since 2009 the theater has partnered with Agência do Bem, and since 2011 has let the agency's Orquestra Nova Sinfonia, a youth orchestra, use its stage for rehearsals.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Criticism

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Barra_da_Tijuca&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Criticism")]

The influence from different countries is criticized by many citizens from the older areas of Rio de Janeiro, especially concerning the 26.8 meters (88 feet) high replica of the [Statue of Liberty](/wiki/Statue_of_Liberty "Statue of Liberty") in the New York City Center.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Barra also has replicas of many international architectural icons like the [Leaning Tower of Pisa](/wiki/Leaning_Tower_of_Pisa "Leaning Tower of Pisa"), the [Tower Bridge](/wiki/Tower_Bridge "Tower Bridge") of London, and the [Eiffel Tower](/wiki/Eiffel_Tower "Eiffel Tower") of Paris (all found in the Barra World Shopping Center).

## In popular culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Barra_da_Tijuca&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: In popular culture")]

Barra da Tijuca (and their luxury condominiums, considered the trademark of Barra da Tijuca) is a recurring theme in Brazilian films, TV series and documentaries, mainly because it portrays (sometimes in a light and funny way, sometimes in a darker and more serious way) the lives of upper-middle class and upper class residents who live there. Among the films and series that take place there are:

* In the Rede Globo telenovela [Laços de Família](/wiki/La%C3%A7os_de_Fam%C3%ADlia "Laços de Família") (2000-2001), Barra da Tijuca is home to the family of Alma Flora Pirajá de Albuquerque ([Marieta Severo](/wiki/Marieta_Severo "Marieta Severo")), her fourth husband Danilo Menezes ([Alexandre Borges](/wiki/Alexandre_Borges "Alexandre Borges")) and her nephew and niece Edu ([Reynaldo Gianecchini](/wiki/Reynaldo_Gianecchini "Reynaldo Gianecchini")) and Estela Monteiro Fernandes ([Júlia Almeida](/wiki/J%C3%BAlia_Almeida "Júlia Almeida")). Barra da Tijuca is portrayed as a highly affluent neighborhood, to an even greater extent than [Leblon](/wiki/Leblon "Leblon"): while the latter is inhabited mostly by apartment-dwelling upper-middle class families with salaried occupations, the family of Alma Flora Pirajá de Albuquerque lives in a large house with a swimming pool and its members enjoy the benefits of inherited wealth.
* The [Rede Globo](/wiki/Rede_Globo "Rede Globo") popular [sitcom](/wiki/Sitcom "Sitcom") *[Toma Lá, Dá Cá](/wiki/Toma_L%C3%A1,_D%C3%A1_C%C3%A1 "Toma Lá, Dá Cá")* (2007-2009), starring [Miguel Falabella](/wiki/Miguel_Falabella "Miguel Falabella") (who is also the co-creator of the series), [Adriana Esteves](/wiki/Adriana_Esteves "Adriana Esteves"), [Marisa Orth](/wiki/Marisa_Orth "Marisa Orth") and Diogo Vilela portrays the lives of the residents of **Jambalaya Ocean Drive**, a high-end condominium from Barra da Tijuca as they get into various fun (and sometimes bizarre and embarrassing) situations, while trying to deal with their dysfunctional families and a crazy manager.
* The 2011/2012 [Rede Globo](/wiki/Rede_Globo "Rede Globo") hit [soap opera](/wiki/Telenovela "Telenovela"), *[Fina Estampa](/wiki/Fina_Estampa "Fina Estampa")*, has Barra da Tijuca as its main setting, as it is there that the characters in the soap opera, mainly the protagonist Griselda "Pereirão" da Silva Pereira (played by [Lília Cabral](/wiki/L%C3%ADlia_Cabral "Lília Cabral")) and the main antagonist, Tereza Cristina Buarque Siqueira de Velmont (played by [Christiane Torloni](/wiki/Christiane_Torloni "Christiane Torloni")) resides and where most of the plot of *Fina Estampa* takes place.
* The 2013 [documentary](/wiki/Documentary "Documentary") *Sorria, Você Está na Barra!* portrays the history, current context and day-to-day life of Barra da Tijuca through some of its most famous residents, such as [Fernando Gabeira](/wiki/Fernando_Gabeira "Fernando Gabeira"), [Adriana Bombom](/wiki/Adriana_Bombom "Adriana Bombom") and [Letícia Spiller](/wiki/Let%C3%ADcia_Spiller "Letícia Spiller").
* The 2014 [drama film](/wiki/Drama_film "Drama film") *Casa Grande*, starring Thales Cavalcanti, [Marcello Novaes](/wiki/Marcello_Novaes "Marcello Novaes") and Suzana Pires, portrays the forbidden romance between an upper-middle-class boy from Barra da Tijuca and a lower-class girl from a *[favela](/wiki/Favela "Favela")* while trying to deal with an economic crisis that affects his family relationship.
* The 2015 [thriller film](/wiki/Thriller_film "Thriller film") *Mate-me por Favor* (*Please Kill Me* in English), starring Valentina Herszage and Dora Freind, depicts the lives of wealthy young people who live there and their lives in [high school](/wiki/High_school "High school") as they try to discover who is behind a [series of brutal murders](/wiki/Serial_killer "Serial killer").
* The 2018 [Amazon Prime Video](/wiki/Amazon_Prime_Video "Amazon Prime Video") documentary *Palace II – 3 Quartos com Vista para o Mar* portrays the story of **[Palace II](/wiki/Palace_II_(building) "Palace II (building)")**, a high-end condominium that collapsed on February 28, 1998 (killing nine people and leaving more than 170 families homeless) and the struggle of the victims' families and survivors in search of justice against those responsible for this tragedy (mainly Sergio Naya, the architect responsible for building Palace II).
* The main character of [Netflix](/wiki/Netflix "Netflix")'s 2021 [teen](/wiki/Teen_film "Teen film") [romantic comedy film](/wiki/Romantic_comedy_film "Romantic comedy film") *Confissões de uma Garota Excluída*, Teanira "Tetê" de Oliveira ([Klara Castanho](/wiki/Klara_Castanho "Klara Castanho")) is a 16-year-old teenage girl from Barra da Tijuca who ends up being forced to move to her grandparents' house in [Copacabana](/wiki/Copacabana,_Rio_de_Janeiro "Copacabana, Rio de Janeiro") ([South Zone of Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/South_Zone_(Rio_de_Janeiro) "South Zone (Rio de Janeiro)")) after her parents became unemployed.
* [Netflix](/wiki/Netflix "Netflix")'s 2022 [comedy-drama](/wiki/Comedy-drama "Comedy-drama") series *Maldivas*, starring [Bruna Marquezine](/wiki/Bruna_Marquezine "Bruna Marquezine"), [Manu Gavassi](/wiki/Manu_Gavassi "Manu Gavassi"), Carol Castro, [Sheron Menezzes](/wiki/Sheron_Menezzes "Sheron Menezzes"), [Vanessa Gerbelli](/wiki/Vanessa_Gerbelli "Vanessa Gerbelli") and Natália Klein (who is also the series' creator) tells the story of a young woman from the interior who finds herself moves to Rio de Janeiro to investigate the suspicious death of her mother and ends up living in **Maldivas**, a luxury condominium located in Barra da Tijuca and whose residents hide secrets that could ruin their apparently perfect lives.
* The 2024 [comedy film](/wiki/Comedy_film "Comedy film") *Mãe, sequestraram a babá*, starring Cláudia Alencar and Letícia Pedro, depicts the story of the kidnapping of Magdalena, an [Argentine cryptocurrency investor based in Brazil](/wiki/Argentine_Brazilians "Argentine Brazilians") and who works as a [nanny](/wiki/Babysitting "Babysitting") for a business couple who live in a luxury condominium in Barra da Tijuca.
* The [Globoplay](/wiki/Globoplay "Globoplay") [anthology series](/wiki/Anthology_series "Anthology series") *[Os Outros](/wiki/The_Others_(Brazilian_TV_series) "The Others (Brazilian TV series)")* has as its main setting for its two seasons the luxury condominiums of Barra da Tijuca and the conflicts between the residents who live there:
  + In the 1st season, the story takes place in the luxury condominium **Barra Diamond**
  + In the 2nd season, the plot takes place in the high-end condominium **Barra Star Dream**.
* Netflix's ongoing [sitcom](/wiki/Sitcom "Sitcom") *A Sogra que te Pariu* has as its main setting a mansion in Barra da Tijuca, as that is where Carlos (Rafael Zulu), the son of the sitcom's protagonist, Dona Isadir (Rodrigo Sant'Anna), lives and where she ends up moving due to the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic").
